general_requirements: Develop Academic Plan (AP) and Academic Plan Committee (APC)
Meet coursework and other general requirements

major_professor.role: Mentor in all aspects of graduate study (research, professional development, funding).
major_professor.policy_update_2023_06_16: Must have a doctoral degree. If not, a co-major professor with a Ph.D. is required.
resources: Resource by Dr. Stephen Gilbert for advising common concerns

timing.deadline: No later than end of first semester of second year

composition_requirements.total_members: At least 5 members of Graduate College Faculty
composition_requirements.hci_members: At least 3 members (including major/co-major professor) from HCI faculty
composition_requirements.outside_member: One member must be outside the major (not an HCI faculty member)

academic_plan.definition: List of courses taken or planned to meet degree requirements.
academic_plan.process: Developed by student and major professor, approved by APC.
academic_plan.approval_deadline_for_prelim: Must be approved by DOGE in Workday at least 3 calendar months before preliminary oral examination.
academic_plan.changes: Allowable; approved by Graduate College, major professor, committee, and DOGE in Workday.
academic_plan.instructions_location: Complete your Academic Plan as a Graduate Student

expired_credits.policy: HCI Graduate Program accepts expired courses on specific Academic Plans.
expired_credits.instructions_location: Petition to Use Expired Credits Toward a Graduate Degree
